Class DataStore

java.lang.Object
photos.model.DataStore
public final class DataStore extends Object
Handles all persistence for the Photos application using Java serialization (ObjectOutputStream / ObjectInputStream).

Directory layout under the working directory: 

  data/
    users.dat        – serialized List<String> of registered usernames
    tagtypes.dat     – serialized Map<String,Boolean> of tag-type → allowsMultiple
    users/
      <username>.dat – serialized User object
    stock/stock/     – image files for the built-in stock album

    Directory holding per-user serialized files.

    Method
    Description
    static void
    deleteUserFile(String username)
    Removes the persisted file for the given username, if it exists.
    static void
    initStockUserIfNeeded()
    Creates the stock user and album from image files in data/stock/stock/ if the stock user has not yet been initialized.

    Deserializes an object of type T from file.

    Loads the tag-type registry (name → allowsMultiple).

    Loads the User object for the given username from disk.

    Returns the list of registered usernames, or an empty list on first run.

    Serializes object to file, creating parent directories as needed.

    Serializes the user's current state (albums + photos + tags) to disk.

  • Field Details

    • USERS_DIR

      public static final Path USERS_DIR
      Directory holding per-user serialized files.

  • Method Details

    • save

      public static void save(Path file, Serializable object) throws IOException
      Serializes object to file, creating parent directories as needed.
      Parameters:
      file - destination path
      object - object to serialize
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs

    • load

      public static <T> T load(Path file, Class<T> type) throws IOException, ClassNotFoundException
      Deserializes an object of type T from file.
      Type Parameters:
      T - expected type
      Parameters:
      file - source path
      type - class token for casting
      Returns:
      deserialized object
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs
      ClassNotFoundException - if the serialized class is not on the classpath

    • loadUsernames

      public static List<String> loadUsernames()
      Returns the list of registered usernames, or an empty list on first run.
      Returns:
      mutable list of usernames

    • saveUsernames

      public static void saveUsernames(List<String> usernames) throws IOException
      Persists the current list of registered usernames.
      Parameters:
      usernames - list to save
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs

    • loadUser

      public static User loadUser(String username)
      Loads the User object for the given username from disk. Returns a new empty User if no file exists yet.
      Parameters:
      username - user to load
      Returns:
      deserialized user, or a fresh empty user

    • saveUser

      public static void saveUser(User user) throws IOException
      Serializes the user's current state (albums + photos + tags) to disk.
      Parameters:
      user - user to persist
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs

    • deleteUserFile

      public static void deleteUserFile(String username)
      Removes the persisted file for the given username, if it exists.
      Parameters:
      username - user whose file should be deleted

    • loadTagTypes

      public static Map<String,Boolean> loadTagTypes()
      Loads the tag-type registry (name → allowsMultiple). Returns the default registry if no file exists yet.
      Returns:
      mutable map of tag types

    • saveTagTypes

      public static void saveTagTypes(Map<String,Boolean> tagTypes) throws IOException
      Persists the tag-type registry.
      Parameters:
      tagTypes - map to save
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs

    • initStockUserIfNeeded

      public static void initStockUserIfNeeded()
      Creates the stock user and album from image files in data/stock/stock/ if the stock user has not yet been initialized. Safe to call on every startup.
